name: Slurm Time Limit Triage
summary: Collect read-only evidence for Slurm time-limit failures.
description: This skill helps users and support teams diagnose Slurm TIMEOUT, walltime exhaustion, and near-time-limit jobs by collecting accounting, job-record, optional efficiency, and log evidence without modifying scheduler state.

tools.name: sacct
tools.notes: Required for completed-job accounting evidence when Slurm accounting is available.

tools.name: scontrol
tools.notes: Optional job record evidence for running, pending, or recently completed jobs.

tools.name: seff
tools.notes: Optional efficiency summary when provided by the site.

inputs.name: job_id
inputs.description: Slurm job id, array task id, or job step id to inspect.

inputs.name: log_file
inputs.description: Optional Slurm stdout, stderr, or application log to scan for time-limit clues.

inputs.name: report_dir
inputs.description: Directory where the read-only evidence bundle should be written.

outputs.name: accounting_report
outputs.description: Slurm sacct table with job and step states, exit codes, elapsed time, time limits, submit/start/end times, resources, and node list.

outputs.name: job_record
outputs.description: Optional scontrol job record for jobs still visible to Slurm.

outputs.name: log_time_signals
outputs.description: Matching log lines for time-limit, signal, checkpoint, workflow retry, and graceful-shutdown symptoms.

outputs.name: triage_summary
outputs.description: Human-readable prompts for deciding whether to request more walltime, checkpoint more often, split work, reduce concurrency, or inspect slow tasks.

tests.description: Validate the manifest and example files.
Check shell syntax for the evidence collection script.
Run against a completed Slurm job with TIMEOUT or near-limit symptoms and confirm the report stays read-only.
